embedding_text: I have designed and shipped production embedding retrieval pipelines that served 50M daily queries with sub-100ms p99 latency, measured against NDCG@10 in offline evaluation. I have worked on modern ML systems, including embeddings, retrieval, ranking, and LLMs, and have experience with vector databases and hybrid search infrastructure. I have a strong background in Python and have hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems, including NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, and A/B test interpretation. I have driven the long-term architecture of candidate-JD matching systems at scale and have mentored junior engineers.
role_summary: This role owns the intelligence layer of Redrob's product, including ranking, retrieval, and matching systems. The ideal candidate will have deep technical depth in modern ML systems and a scrappy product-engineering attitude, with the ability to ship working systems quickly and iterate based on real user feedback.
hard_requirements: Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems deployed to real users, including handling embedding drift, index refresh, and retrieval-quality regression
Production experience with vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure, including operational experience with technologies like Pinecone, Weaviate, or OpenSearch
Strong Python skills, with a focus on code quality
Hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems, including NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, and A/B test interpretation
nice_to_have_requirements: LLM fine-tuning experience, including LoRA, QLoRA, or PEFT
Experience with learning-to-rank models, including XGBoost-based or neural models
Prior exposure to HR-tech, recruiting tech, or marketplace products
Background in distributed systems or large-scale inference optimization
Open-source contributions in the AI/ML space
disqualifier_patterns: Entire career spent in pure research environments without production deployment experience
AI experience limited to recent projects using LangChain to call OpenAI, without substantial pre-LLM-era ML production experience
Senior engineers who haven't written production code in the last 18 months due to 'architecture' or 'tech lead' roles
Title-chasers who optimize for title progression by switching companies every 1.5 years
Framework enthusiasts who focus on frameworks rather than systems
Candidates with no product-company experience, only working at consulting firms
Candidates with primary expertise in computer vision, speech, or robotics without significant NLP/IR exposure
Candidates who have only worked on closed-source proprietary systems for 5+ years without external validation
preferred_location: Pune/Noida, India (Hybrid — flexible cadence), with openness to relocation candidates from Tier-1 Indian cities
notice_preference: Sub-30-day notice, with the ability to buy out up to 30 days
evaluation_guidance: When evaluating candidates, prioritize those who have demonstrated production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems, vector databases, and evaluation frameworks. Nice-to-have items should increase a candidate's score only if their hard requirements are already met. Be cautious of title-chasers, framework enthusiasts, and candidates with limited production experience. Consider behavioral signals, such as login activity and recruiter response rate, when evaluating candidate availability.
